query: Estimating running costs for a Nissan Leaf over five years in Japan
brand_topic_category: Value, offers, finance and total cost of ownership

request_params_without_api_key.q: Estimating running costs for a Nissan Leaf over five years in Japan

search_parameters.q: Estimating running costs for a Nissan Leaf over five years in Japan

text_blocks.snippet: Estimating the running costs for a Nissan Leaf over five years in Japan involves accounting for electricity, mandatory inspections, annual taxes, insurance, and maintenance.

Over a 5-year period with average driving distances, a Nissan Leaf will cost approximately ¥750,000 to ¥1,150,000 ($5,000 to $7,700 USD) in total running costs, excluding optional costs like monthly parking space rentals or highway tolls.
A detailed overview of the mandatory and recurring operational expenses reveals the following breakdown:

table: Assumes 10,000 km/year at residential electricity rates.
Flat annual rate for zero-emission/electric vehicles.
Required every 2 years; occurs twice in a 5-year window.
Paid during Shaken. EVs get partial eco-reductions.
Highly dependent on age, gold license status, and provider.
Includes 12-month inspections, cabin filters, wiper blades, and 1 set of new tires.

detailed.snippet: Assumes 10,000 km/year at residential electricity rates.
Flat annual rate for zero-emission/electric vehicles.
Required every 2 years; occurs twice in a 5-year window.
Paid during Shaken. EVs get partial eco-reductions.
Highly dependent on age, gold license status, and provider.
Includes 12-month inspections, cabin filters, wiper blades, and 1 set of new tires.

formatted.notes_details: Assumes 10,000 km/year at residential electricity rates.
Flat annual rate for zero-emission/electric vehicles.
Required every 2 years; occurs twice in a 5-year window.
Paid during Shaken. EVs get partial eco-reductions.
Highly dependent on age, gold license status, and provider.
Includes 12-month inspections, cabin filters, wiper blades, and 1 set of new tires.

text_blocks.snippet: Assuming a standard Japanese driving average of 10,000 km per year (50,000 km total over 5 years):

list.snippet: Efficiency: The Nissan Leaf averages roughly 6.5 km to 7 km per kWh. Driving 10,000 km requires approximately $1,450 \text{ kWh}$ annually.
Rates: Following the expiration of Japanese government energy subsidies, household electricity rates sit at an average of ¥31 to ¥35 per kWh depending on the region and time-of-day plan.

Formula:
$\text{Annual\ Cost}=\left(\frac{10,000\text{\ km}}{6.8\text{\ km/kWh}}\right)\times \text{\yen 33/kWh}\approx \text{\yen 48,500\ per\ year}$

text_blocks.snippet: In Japan, the annual automobile tax is calculated based on engine displacement.

list.snippet: Because electric vehicles have no combustion engine, they are categorized in the lowest bracket (<1.0L displacement).
The standard rate for an EV registered after October 2019 is a flat ¥25,000 per year, totaling ¥125,000 over five years.

text_blocks.snippet: Vehicles in Japan must undergo a rigorous safety inspection (Shaken) every 2 years. For a 5-year ownership period, you will pay for this twice (at Year 2 and Year 4).

list.snippet: Shaken Legal Fees & Labor: Approximately ¥40,000 to ¥50,000 per inspection loop, assuming no major mechanical failures.

Weight Tax (Juryo-zei): The Nissan Leaf weighs between 1,520 kg and 1,680 kg, placing it in the 1.5 to 2.0-ton bracket. The standard tax rate is ¥12,000 per 2 years. However, Japan's Eco-Car tax benefits offer a 50% to 75% reduction for EVs. Expect to pay roughly ¥4,500 to ¥6,000 per inspection cycle.
Compulsory Insurance (Jidousha Baiseki): Legally mandated and paid alongside your Shaken cycle. It costs roughly ¥18,000 to ¥20,000 for 24 months of coverage.

Voluntary Insurance (Nyui Hoken): Crucial for comprehensive coverage. For an experienced driver with a "Gold" license status, rates hover around ¥35,000 to ¥50,000 per year. Younger drivers or lower-tier licenses can see this double.

text_blocks.snippet: The Nissan Leaf has significantly fewer moving parts than an internal combustion vehicle, saving money on oil changes, spark plugs, and timing belts.

list.snippet: Annual Checks: Mandatory 12-month inspections cost roughly ¥10,000 to ¥15,000 at local shops.
Consumables: Brake pads last much longer due to regenerative braking. Expect minor costs for cabin air filters, brake fluid flushes, and windshield wipers.
Tires: Due to the instant torque and heavy battery weight of EVs, front tires degrade faster. Budget for one complete set of replacement tires over the 5-year span (¥50,000 to ¥70,000).

Parking Space (Shako Shoumeisho): If you do not own a house with a driveway, renting a parking space is legally required to register the vehicle. Depending on the prefecture, this can add ¥5,000 to ¥30,000+ per month to your baseline costs.

Battery Degradation: If buying a used older-generation Leaf (24 kWh or 30 kWh models), battery capacity loss will decrease your effective range per charge. While it does not change immediate registration or tax costs, it will increase the frequency of daytime charging stops.

text_blocks.snippet: If you would like to fine-tune this estimation, please share:

list.snippet: Whether you plan to buy the Leaf new or used.
Your prefecture or city in Japan (to calculate exact parking and electricity provider metrics).

serpapi_response.reconstructed_markdown:
Estimating the running costs for a Nissan Leaf over five years in Japan involves accounting for electricity, mandatory inspections, annual taxes, insurance, and maintenance. [15] [16]

Over a 5\-year period with average driving distances, a Nissan Leaf will cost approximately ¥750,000 to ¥1,150,000 \($5,000 to $7,700 USD\) in total running costs, excluding optional costs like monthly parking space rentals or highway tolls.

### Cost Breakdown Over 5 Years

A detailed overview of the mandatory and recurring operational expenses reveals the following breakdown:

| Cost Category | Annual Average Cost | 5\-Year Total Estimate | Notes / Details |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Electricity \(Fuel\) | ¥35,000 – ¥50,000 | ¥175,000 – ¥250,000 | Assumes 10,000 km/year at residential electricity rates. |
| Automobile Tax | ¥25,000 | ¥125,000 | Flat annual rate for zero\-emission/electric vehicles. |
| Shaken \(Inspection\) | ¥40,000 – ¥50,000 | ¥80,000 – ¥100,000 | Required every 2 years; occurs twice in a 5\-year window. |
| Weight Tax \(Juryo\-zei\) | ¥4,500 – ¥6,000 | ¥22,500 – ¥30,000 | Paid during Shaken. EVs get partial eco\-reductions. |
| Voluntary Insurance | ¥35,000 – ¥65,000 | ¥175,000 – ¥325,000 | Highly dependent on age, gold license status, and provider. |
| Maintenance & Tires | ¥35,000 – ¥60,000 | ¥175,000 – ¥300,000 | Includes 12\-month inspections, cabin filters, wiper blades, and 1 set of new tires. |
| Total Estimated Cost | ¥150,000 – ¥231,000 | ¥752,500 – ¥1,130,000 | Final 5\-year operational budget envelope. |
[2] [12] [8] [11] [14] [10] [13]

### Step\-by\-Step Expense Analysis


### Variable Factor Warnings

- Parking Space \(Shako Shoumeisho\): If you do not own a house with a driveway, renting a parking space is legally required to register the vehicle. Depending on the prefecture, this can add ¥5,000 to ¥30,000\+ per month to your baseline costs.
- Battery Degradation: If buying a used older\-generation Leaf \(24 kWh or 30 kWh models\), battery capacity loss will decrease your effective range per charge. While it does not change immediate registration or tax costs, it will increase the frequency of daytime charging stops.

If you would like to fine\-tune this estimation, please share:

- Whether you plan to buy the Leaf new or used.
- Your prefecture or city in Japan \(to calculate exact parking and electricity provider metrics\).
- Your estimated annual driving mileage.
